div.head: The Secretary of State to The Chargé in Germany (Morris)
div.p: 1396. Your 1903, May 14. Following for MacVeagh in reply to his 301, May 6.

184. Department is unable provide funds or shipping for repatriation for the large number of Americans in the Mediterranean area. Upon receipt from you of information as to names, addresses and amounts desired, it will gladly communicate with relatives, friends or employers here of Americans in Greece needing funds for subsistence or repatriation expenses and to endeavor to transmit those funds if received.
You should proceed to Washington for consultation at your early convenience.The Minister in Greece left Athens, June 5, 1941, for Vienna, en route to the United States. Formal travel order follows.
